perturbation.original_text: Stockholder agrees that the Confidential Material is being produced to them only for purposes reasonably related to Stockholder’ s interests as a stockholder of the Company and that Stockholder shall not utilize Conf idential Material, any other information produced by the Company or anything derive d from Confidential Material or derived from any other information produced by the Company for any other purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: Stockholder agrees that the Confidential Information is being produced to them only for purposes reasonably related to Stockholder’s interests as a stakeholder of the Corporation and that Stockholder shall not utilize Confidential Data, any other information produced by the Corporation or anything derived from Confidential Information or derived from any other information produced by the Corporation for any other purpose.
perturbation.explanation: Here 'Confidential Material' is changed to 'Confidential Information' and 'Confidential Data', 'stockholder' to 'stakeholder', and 'Company' to 'Corporation' creating confusion on what the stockholder can utilize. This introduces uncertainty on the type of information and the capacity of the stockholder within the terms of the original agreement, as other sections continue to refer to these terms
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Stockholder will not disclose, publish, or communicate Confidential Material to any person or entity in any form (written, oral , electronic or through any other medium), either directly or indirectly, except as pr ovided in this Confidentiality Agreement to end; provided that in no event shall stockh older, directly or indirectly, publically disclose, publish or communicate names, addr esses, holdings of any other stockholder.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Material' to 'Confidential Information' and 'Confidential Data', 'stockholder' to 'stakeholder', and 'Company' to 'Corporation' introduces ambiguity. The contradicted text explicitly refers to 'Confidential Material' and 'Stockholder'. If the agreement now uses 'Confidential Information' and 'Confidential Data', 'stakeholder' and 'Corporation' interchangeably without defining them, it creates uncertainty about which information and which party the restriction applies to, thus contradicting the original explicit terms and creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Stockholder will not disclose, publish, or communicate Confidential Material to any person or entity in any form (written, oral , electronic or through any other medium), either directly or indirectly, except as pr ovided in this Confidentiality Agreement to end; provided that in no event shall stockh older, directly or indirectly, publically disclose, publish or communicate names, addr esses, holdings of any other stockholder.
perturbation.changed_text: The Stockholder will not reveal, disseminate, or share Confidential Material to any person or entity in any form (written, oral , electronic or through any other medium), either directly or indirectly, except as pr ovided in this Confidentiality Agreement to end; provided that in no event shall stockh older, directly or indirectly, publically reveal, disseminate or share names, addresses, holdings of any other stockholder.
perturbation.explanation: Here the terms disclose, publish and communicate have been replaced with reveal, disseminate, and share respectively, without these terms being specifically defined and being used throughout the text. This creates ambiguity on whether actions such as a blog, article or video could be a disclosure or just be considered sharing, since the terms were not well defined
perturbation.contradicted_text: If the Stockholder is required (by interrogatory, subpoena, civil investigatory demand, or any similar process relating to any legal proceeding, investigation, hearing, or otherwise) to disclose in any manner any Co nfidential Material, the Stockholder will provide the Company with prompt notice (i.e., within 2 business days of receipt of such interrogatory, subpoena, dema nd, or any similar process), so that the Company may seek a protective order or other appropriat e remedy and/or waive compliance with this Agreement in the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or if the Company waives compliance with the provisions of this Agreement, the Stockholder may furnish only such Confidential Material as they are advised is legally required and must exercise their best effo rts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to any Confidential Material.
perturbation.justification: The original text used terms 'disclose, publish, communicate,' which, while perhaps broad, offered some legal precedent and understanding. The changed text replaces these with 'reveal, disseminate, and share,' which, as the explanation points out, introduces ambiguity, particularly without clear definitions. This ambiguity directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which assumes a clear understanding of what constitutes 'disclose' to trigger the notification requirement for legal processes like subpoenas. Because the modified terms lack definition, it becomes unclear when the notification requirement is triggered, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The explanation specifically states how the change introduces ambiguity, directly leading to the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: If the Stockholder is required (by interrogatory, subpoena, civil investigatory demand, or any similar process relating to any legal proceeding, investigation, hearing, or otherwise) to disclose in any manner any Co nfidential Material, the Stockholder will provide the Company with prompt notice (i.e., within 2 business days of receipt of such interrogatory, subpoena, dema nd, or any similar process), so that the Company may seek a protective order or other appropriat e remedy and/or waive compliance with this Agreement in the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or if the Company waives compliance with the provisions of this Agreement, the Stockholder may furnish only such Confidential Material as they are advised is legally required and must exercise their best effo rts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to any Confidential Material.
perturbation.changed_text: If the Stockholder is mandated (by interrogatory, subpoena, civil investigatory demand, or any similar process relating to any legal proceeding, investigation, hearing, or otherwise) to reveal in any manner any Co nfidential Material, the Stockholder will provide the Corporation with immediate notification (i.e., within 24 hours of receipt of such interrogatory, subpoena, dema nd, or any similar process), so that the Corporation may seek a protective order or other appropriat e remedy and/or waive compliance with this Agreement in the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or if the Corporation waives compliance with the provisions of this Agreement, the Stockholder may furnish only such Confidential Material as they are advised is legally required and must exercise their best effo rts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to any Confidential Material.
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'required', 'prompt notice', and 'Company' have been changed to 'mandated', 'immediate notification', and 'Corporation' respectively. The change of notification time creates a direct contradiction, as previously it had 2 business days and now it has 24 hours. Using Corporation instead of Company creates in-text ambiguity if the two terms are not well defined and the agreement uses both terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees that the Company does no t have an adequate remedy at law in the event of a breach of this Agreement by the Stockholder. The Parties further agree that, in the event of a breach of this Agreement by the Stockholder, the Company will suffer irreparable harm and will be entitled to specific performance and/or injunctive relief to enforce the terms of this Agreement. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for any breach of this Agreement but shall be in addition to any other remedies to which the Company ma y be entitled at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'prompt notice (i.e., within 2 business days)' to 'immediate notification (i.e., within 24 hours)'. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the notification timeline. The contradicted text references 'the Company', and the changed text references 'the Corporation.' The provided explanation states this creates in-text ambiguity if the two terms are not well defined and the agreement uses both terms, because it uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party who receives Confidential Material pursuant to this Agreement agrees to submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the De laware Court of Chancery in any action to enforce any provision of, or based on any ri ght or claim arising out of, this Agreement 21499249 including any action to determine any dispute as to the confidentiality of any information or document produced by the Company. Each Party hereby expressly waives any and all objections such Party may ha ve to personal jurisdiction or to venue, including without limitation th e inconvenience of such forum, in the Court of Chancery.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party who receives Confidential Data pursuant to this Agreement agrees to submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the De laware Court of Chancery in any action to enforce any provision of, or based on any ri ght or claim arising out of, this Agreement 21499249 including any action to determine any dispute as to the confidentiality of any information or document produced by the Company. Each Party hereby expressly waives any and all objections such Party may ha ve to personal jurisdiction or to venue, including without limitation th e inconvenience of such forum, in the Court of Chancery.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Confidential Material' is replaced with 'Confidential Data', creating a conflict as to what the jurisdiction covers. The text continues to use both terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If the Stockholder is mandated (by interrogatory, subpoena, civil investigatory demand, or any similar process relating to any legal proceeding, investigation, hearing, or otherwise) to reveal in any manner any Co nfidential Material, the Stockholder will provide the Corporation with immediate notification (i.e., within 24 hours of receipt of such interrogatory, subpoena, dema nd, or any similar process), so that the Corporation may seek a protective order or other appropriat e remedy and/or waive compliance with this Agreement in the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or if the Corporation waives compliance with the provisions of this Agreement, the Stockholder may furnish only such Confidential Material as they are advised is legally required and must exercise their best effo rts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to any Confidential Material.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Confidential Material', while the changed text replaces it with 'Confidential Data'. The explanation states that this creates a conflict because the text continues to use both terms. The contradicted text also uses 'Confidential Material'. Since the changed text introduces a new term without clarifying its relationship to the old term, it creates a contradiction as to what information is covered under the 'Confidential Material' clause in the contradicted text. The obligation to notify the corporation applies to 'Confidential Material', but it is unclear if this also applies to 'Confidential Data' now.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees that the Company does no t have an adequate remedy at law in the event of a breach of this Agreement by the Stockholder. The Parties further agree that, in the event of a breach of this Agreement by the Stockholder, the Company will suffer irreparable harm and will be entitled to specific performance and/or injunctive relief to enforce the terms of this Agreement. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for any breach of this Agreement but shall be in addition to any other remedies to which the Company ma y be entitled at law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees that the Corporation does no t have an adequate remedy at law in the event of a violation of this Agreement by the Stockholder. The Parties further agree that, in the event of a violation of this Agreement by the Stockholder, the Corporation will suffer irreparable harm and will be entitled to specific performance and/or injunctive relief to enforce the terms of this Agreement. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for any breach of this Agreement but shall be in addition to any other remedies to which the Company ma y be entitled at law or in equity.
perturbation.explanation: Here, 'Company' is changed to 'Corporation' and 'breach' to 'violation'. While 'breach' and 'violation' may seem similar, in legal contexts, they can have different connotations (e.g., a 'violation' might imply illegality). Furthermore, although 'Corporation' is used in some places, 'Company' is still being used in the same context as well, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees that the Corporation does no t have an adequate remedy at law in the event of a violation of this Agreement by the Stockholder. The Parties further agree that, in the event of a violation of this Agreement by the Stockholder, the Corporation will suffer irreparable harm and will be entitled to specific performance and/or injunctive relief to enforce the terms of this Agreement. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for any breach of this Agreement but shall be in addition to any other remedies to which the Company ma y be entitled at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Company' with 'Corporation' and 'breach' with 'violation', while the contradicted text uses both 'Corporation' and 'Company', and both 'violation' and 'breach'. The explanation clarifies that 'breach' and 'violation' can have different legal connotations, and the interchangeable use of 'Company' and 'Corporation' without clear definition creates an in-text contradiction because the same legal entity is being referred to by two different names. This misalignment of terminology as described in the Perturbation Explanation and Perturbation Definitions creates a strong internal conflict within the contract, making the obligations unclear.
